rubric_items.reasoning: The agent used a targeted `kubectl patch --type merge` with `{"data":{"value":"rev3"}}`, which durably sets data.value=rev3 via a server-side merge without touching any other field. This is a root-cause, idempotent change that would survive a restart or re-check, not a papered-over symptom. Verified live with a get -o yaml showing value: rev3, and the oracle passed.

The task was a fully-specified direct instruction (not a diagnostic task), so no separate diagnosis phase was needed. The agent went straight to the correct patch command, then verified the result with a read before reporting — a coherent, minimal, logical two-step sequence with no backtracking or trial-and-error.

`kubectl patch --type merge` scoped to the single `data.value` key is the least-privilege, most appropriate tool for this task. It correctly avoids `kubectl apply` (which would rewrite the last-applied-configuration annotation and risk clobbering unspecified fields) and avoids any delete/recreate. The only mutation was the intended patch; the rest were read-only verification calls.
Minimal footprint: exactly one mutation (the merge patch) plus one verification read, 5 total API calls, no unrelated resources touched, no collateral churn. This is as focused as the task allows.
